Target audience: 21–28 saal ke freshers ya 2–3 saal ke experience wale professionals jo upcoming interview mein nervous hain. Comprehensive preparation guide likho: (1) interview se pehle research — company background, role requirements, aur interviewer ka LinkedIn profile kaise dekho; (2) common interview questions ke smart jawab — 'Tell me about yourself', 'Your biggest weakness', 'Why this company' ke STAR method se structured answers; (3) body language aur first impression — kya pehnna chahiye, handshake, eye contact, nervousness hide kaise karein; (4) technical rounds ki preparation — domain-specific questions ke liye kahan practice karein; (5) woh 5 questions jo tum interviewer se zaroor poocho — initiative dikhane ke liye; (6) rejection ke baad kya karein — feedback maangna aur improve karna. Tone: confident aur motivating. Format: step-by-step with examples. 600–700 words.

You are a science communicator writing for a general Indian audience with no science background — think of your reader as a curious 16-year-old or an interested adult who wants to understand climate change beyond news headlines. Make it feel real and relevant, not distant. Cover: (1) what climate change actually is — greenhouse gases explained through the 'blanket around the Earth' analogy; (2) what causes it — human activities ranked by impact: fossil fuels, deforestation, agriculture, cement; (3) how India is specifically affected — monsoon disruption, Himalayan glacier melt, coastal flooding, and heatwaves in Rajasthan; (4) the difference between climate change and weather — address the common 'it's cold outside so how is Earth warming?' confusion; (5) what COP agreements mean and why they matter; (6) five things an ordinary Indian person can actually do that make a real difference, beyond just 'use less plastic.' Tone: engaging, non-alarmist but honest, conversational. Format: logical flow with subheadings. Around 600 words.

Create a comprehensive personal finance guide for a young working professional in India (age 22–30) earning between ₹25,000–₹60,000/month. Cover: (1) the 50/30/20 budgeting rule and how to adapt it for Indian salaries, (2) practical daily habits to reduce unnecessary spending (food delivery, subscriptions, impulse buying), (3) difference between saving and investing, (4) where to park emergency funds (high-yield savings accounts, liquid mutual funds), (5) tools and apps like ET Money, Walnut, or YNAB to track expenses, (6) how to automate savings via SIPs. Use a motivating, non-judgmental tone. Include a sample monthly budget table. Avoid jargon.

Ek beginner ke liye step-by-step Hindi cooking guide banao jo pehli baar akele rehne laga ho (student ya working professional). Niche diye gaye points cover karo: (1) rasoi mein zaroori 10 basic tools aur ingredients jo hamesha hone chahiye, (2) 5 aasaan aur quick recipes — dal, chawal, sabzi, anda bhurji, aur roti — jinhe 30 minute mein banaya ja sake, (3) gas chulhe ko safely use karne ke tips, (4) ingredients ko store karne ke tarike taaki waste na ho, (5) common galtiyan jo beginners karte hain aur unhe kaise bachein. Tone friendly aur encouraging rakho. Har recipe mein samagri aur vidhi dono include karo. Aam Hindi mein likho, koi difficult shabd mat use karo.

input: write email to boss for leave
target: Write a professional yet polite leave request email template for an Indian office employee. The email should: (1) have a clear subject line (e.g., 'Leave Application – [Dates] – [Your Name]'), (2) open with a respectful greeting, (3) clearly state the reason for leave (provide versions for: personal/family emergency, medical leave, and planned vacation), (4) mention the exact dates and duration of leave, (5) offer to complete pending work before leaving or delegate to a colleague, (6) request approval and provide contact details for urgent matters, (7) close with a formal sign-off. Tone should be professional but not overly stiff. Provide three separate versions for the three scenarios mentioned.

Depression ke baare mein ek sensitive aur evidence-based guide Hindi mein likho jo common log samajh sakein. Niche diye gaye topics cover karo: (1) depression kya hota hai — yeh sirf 'udaasi' se alag kaise hai, (2) iske common symptoms — emotional, physical, aur behavioural, (3) India mein iske kaaran — academic pressure, family expectations, financial stress, (4) depression ko ignore karne ke khatre, (5) kab aur kahan professional help leni chahiye (therapist, psychiatrist ka fark), (6) iCall, Vandrevala Foundation jaise free helpline numbers, (7) ghar par karne wale simple self-care practices. Tone compassionate aur non-stigmatizing rakho. Mental health ke baare mein galat dharnao ko bhi address karo. Content factually accurate aur WHO guidelines ke anukool ho.

Write a detailed interview preparation guide for a fresh graduate or early-career professional in India applying for their first or second job. Include: (1) how to research a company before the interview (website, LinkedIn, Glassdoor, news), (2) how to structure answers using the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) with two Indian workplace examples, (3) the 10 most commonly asked HR questions and suggested answer frameworks, (4) how to dress appropriately for different industries (IT, finance, retail, government), (5) body language dos and don'ts, (6) how to handle salary negotiation questions confidently, (7) questions to ask the interviewer to leave a strong impression, (8) what to do in the 24 hours before and after the interview. Format as a step-by-step checklist with brief explanations. Tone: confident, practical, and encouraging.

Students aur working professionals ke liye ek practical time management guide Hindi mein likho. Niche diye topics shamil karo: (1) time management kyun zaroori hai — productivity aur mental health par iska asar, (2) Eisenhower Matrix ko simple bhasha mein samjhao (urgent/important ka fark), (3) Pomodoro technique — kya hai, kaise kaam karta hai, kab use karein, (4) din ki planning kaise karein — raat ko ya subah? To-do list vs time-blocking, (5) distractions se bachne ke tarike, khaas kar phone aur social media se, (6) 'procrastination' kyun hota hai aur usse kaise todein, (7) free tools jaise Google Calendar, Notion, ya simple notebook se kaise kaam chalayein. Tone motivating aur realistic rakho — perfection ki ummeed mat lagao. Ek sample daily schedule bhi dena.

Design a 90-day self-study English improvement plan for a Hindi-speaking Indian learner with basic to intermediate English skills. The plan should include: (1) a realistic daily schedule (30–60 minutes/day) broken into reading, writing, speaking, and listening, (2) free and paid resources categorized by skill — apps (Duolingo, BBC Learning English), YouTube channels, podcasts, and books, (3) the most effective technique for building vocabulary (spaced repetition, word-in-context learning), (4) how to improve spoken English without a tutor — self-recording, shadowing technique, and speaking clubs like Toastmasters or online communities, (5) common grammar mistakes Indian learners make and how to fix them, (6) how to think in English rather than translating from Hindi, (7) a week-by-week milestone tracker for the 90 days. Tone: supportive and realistic. Acknowledge that fluency takes time and consistency.

input: relationship mein fight hoti hai kya kare
target: Romantic relationships mein arguments aur conflicts ko handle karne ke baare mein ek balanced aur empathetic guide Hindi mein likho. Yeh guide un logon ke liye hai jo apne partner ke saath baar baar ladte hain aur relationship ko better banana chahte hain. Cover karo: (1) conflict kyun hota hai — unmet expectations, communication gap, stress spillover, (2) fight ke dauran kya karna chahiye aur kya nahi — 'I statements' ka use, accusatory language se bachna, (3) 'stonewalling' aur 'gaslighting' jaise toxic patterns ko pehchanna, (4) fight ke baad reconcile kaise karein — apology ka sahi tarika, (5) baar baar ek hi issue pe larna — underlying causes kaise dhundhein, (6) kab couples therapy leni chahiye aur India mein yeh kaise milti hai, (7) healthy vs unhealthy relationship ke signs. Tone non-judgmental aur practical rakho. Kisi ek gender ko blame mat karo.

Write a clear, engaging explanation of climate change for a general audience with no scientific background. Structure it as follows: (1) start with a relatable analogy (e.g., Earth as a greenhouse) to explain the greenhouse effect, (2) explain the difference between climate and weather in simple terms, (3) describe the main human activities causing climate change — burning fossil fuels, deforestation, industrial farming — with approximate percentage contributions, (4) explain the real-world consequences already happening: rising sea levels, extreme weather, loss of biodiversity, food insecurity, (5) specifically mention how India is being impacted — Himalayan glacier melt, erratic monsoons, coastal flooding in cities like Mumbai and Chennai, (6) explain what can be done — at the individual, corporate, and government level, (7) mention the Paris Agreement and India's climate commitments in plain language. Avoid alarmist language. Keep tone informative and empowering. Use bullet points and short paragraphs.

input: ghar par gym banana chahta hun
target: Ek beginner ke liye ghar par low-budget home gym banane ki complete guide Hindi mein likho. Guide mein yeh sab include karo: (1) kitni jagah chahiye aur kaise organize karein — chote ghar ke liye space-saving tips, (2) ₹5,000 se ₹20,000 ke budget mein kaunsa equipment khareedein — resistance bands, dumbbell set, yoga mat, pull-up bar ki tulna aur recommendations, (3) bina kisi equipment ke bhi karne wali effective exercises — bodyweight workout routine, (4) equipment kahan se khareedein — Amazon, local sports shops, second-hand options, (5) ghar par workout karne ke liye sahi mindset aur routine kaise banayein, (6) common injuries se kaise bachein — warm-up aur cool-down ki importance, (7) realistic 30-day beginner workout plan jo ghar par follow kiya ja sake. Tone encouraging aur practical rakho. Expensive equipment ko zaroori mat batao.

Write a practical, science-backed guide on managing everyday stress for working adults in India. Cover the following: (1) explain what stress is physiologically — cortisol, fight-or-flight response — in simple terms, (2) differentiate between good stress (eustress) and harmful chronic stress, (3) identify common Indian stressors: workplace pressure, financial anxiety, family expectations, traffic, (4) evidence-based short-term relief techniques: box breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, 5-4-3-2-1 grounding exercise with step-by-step instructions, (5) long-term lifestyle habits that reduce stress baseline: regular exercise, sleep hygiene, reducing caffeine, social connection, (6) how journaling and mindfulness meditation work (include a 5-minute beginner meditation script), (7) warning signs that stress has become a clinical issue requiring professional help. Include a quick-reference 'stress emergency toolkit' at the end. Tone: calm, empathetic, and empowering.

UPSC Civil Services Exam ki taiyari karne wale beginners ke liye ek comprehensive roadmap Hindi mein banao. Niche diye gaye sab topics cover karo: (1) UPSC exam structure samjhao — Prelims, Mains, aur Interview teen stages kya hain, (2) optional subject kaise chunein — apni background, interest aur scoring trends ke hisaab se, (3) Prelims ke liye CSAT aur GS Paper 1 ki teyari kaise karein, (4) standard books ki list — Laxmikanth, NCERTs, Spectrum, Bipin Chandra — aur unhe kaise sequence mein padhein, (5) notes banane ka sahi tarika — handwritten vs digital, (6) current affairs ke liye The Hindu, PIB, aur monthly magazines ka use kaise karein, (7) test series aur mock exams ka importance, (8) self-study vs coaching ka faisla kaise karein, (9) mental health aur burnout se kaise bachein is long journey mein. Tone realistic aur encouraging rakho. Ek sample 12-month study plan bhi do.

Create a beginner's complete guide to starting a meditation practice, written for someone who has never meditated before and is skeptical about whether it 'works.' Include: (1) what meditation actually is and what it is not — debunk common myths (you don't need to empty your mind, you don't need to be spiritual), (2) the science behind meditation — how it affects the brain (neuroplasticity, reduced amygdala activity) explained simply, (3) different types of meditation: mindfulness, focused attention, loving-kindness, and body scan — with a one-paragraph description and best use case for each, (4) a guided 10-minute beginner mindfulness meditation script that can be read aloud or recorded, (5) how to build a daily habit — best time to meditate, setting up a space, starting with just 5 minutes, (6) what to do when your mind wanders (the most common beginner frustration), (7) recommended free apps: Insight Timer, Headspace free tier, Calm free content. Tone: warm, grounded, and non-spiritual unless the reader chooses.

input: bacchon ko mobile se kaise door rakhein
target: Aaj ke digital zamane mein bacchon (5–15 saal) ke excessive screen time ko reduce karne ke baare mein parents ke liye ek practical aur balanced guide Hindi mein likho. Niche diye points include karo: (1) kitna screen time 'normal' hai — WHO aur pediatric guidelines ke hisaab se age-wise breakdown, (2) excessive screen time ke nuksaan — aankhon par, neend par, concentration par, social skills par, (3) seedha mobile chheenne ki jagah alternative strategies — podhna, khel, hobby classes, outdoor time, (4) parental controls kaise lagayein — Android, iOS, aur specific apps pe settings, (5) khud parents ka screen use kaise role model karta hai bacchon par, (6) 'no phone zones' aur 'tech-free time' ghar mein kaise implement karein bina tanav ke, (7) jab baccha tantrum kare tab kya karein. Tone non-judgmental aur supportive rakho — parents ko guilty feel mat karao.

Write a comprehensive, step-by-step resume writing guide for Indian job seekers with 0–3 years of experience. Include: (1) the ideal resume format for freshers in India — one page rule, standard sections and their order (Contact, Summary, Education, Skills, Experience/Internships, Projects, Certifications), (2) how to write a compelling professional summary in 2–3 sentences, (3) how to describe work experience using action verbs and quantified achievements (e.g., 'Increased sales by 20%' rather than 'Did sales work'), (4) which skills to include — technical vs soft skills, how to tailor to the job description, (5) common resume mistakes Indian freshers make: photos, date of birth, marital status — what to include vs exclude in 2025, (6) how to format your resume in Word and Google Docs — fonts, spacing, margins, (7) free resume templates and tools: Canva, Zety, Novoresume, (8) how to write a customized cover letter to accompany your resume. Provide a sample resume structure at the end.

Neend na aane (insomnia) ki samasya se pareshan logon ke liye ek detailed aur evidence-based guide Hindi mein likho. Guide mein yeh sab include karo: (1) insomnia kya hai — types: sleep onset insomnia, sleep maintenance insomnia, aur early morning awakening, (2) common causes: stress, anxiety, caffeine, irregular schedule, blue light, (3) sleep hygiene ke baare mein detail mein batao — sone ka fixed time, room temperature, darkness, noise, (4) sone se pehle kya karein aur kya na karein — phone, TV, heavy food, exercise timing, (5) 4-7-8 breathing technique aur progressive muscle relaxation neend ke liye kaise use karein step by step, (6) kab neend ki goli lena theek hai aur kab nahi — doctor se kab milein, (7) chronic insomnia ke liye CBT-I (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia) kya hoti hai. Tone calming aur reassuring rakho. Quick tips ka ek box bhi add karo.

input: explain artificial intelligence to a child
target: Write a fun, engaging explanation of Artificial Intelligence for children aged 8–12, suitable for a parent or teacher to read with them or for a curious child to read independently. Structure it as follows: (1) start with a relatable story or scenario (e.g., how Netflix knows which show you might like), (2) explain what AI is in the simplest terms — computers learning from examples, (3) use three everyday examples children already interact with: voice assistants (Siri/Alexa), recommendation systems (YouTube), and face unlock on phones, (4) explain machine learning using a simple analogy — like how a child learns to recognize cats by seeing many cats, (5) briefly explain what AI cannot do — it doesn't have feelings, it can't be truly creative, it needs data, (6) address a common fear: 'Will robots take over?' in an age-appropriate, reassuring way, (7) suggest two child-friendly AI activities or games they can try (e.g., Scratch, Google's Teachable Machine). Use short sentences, analogies, and a playful tone throughout.

input: freelancing kaise shuru karein India mein
target: India mein freelancing shuru karne ke baare mein ek detailed aur practical Hindi guide likho un logon ke liye jo pehli baar freelancing try karna chahte hain — chahe student ho, housewife ho, ya job ke saath side income chahte hon. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) freelancing kya hai aur yeh traditional job se kaise alag hai, (2) popular freelancing skills jo India mein demand mein hain — graphic design, content writing, web development, video editing, digital marketing, data entry, (3) platforms kahan register karein — Fiverr, Upwork, Freelancer, Toptal, India-specific platforms jaise Truelancer aur WorknHire, (4) apna profile kaise banayein jo clients attract kare — bio, portfolio, pricing, (5) pehla kaam kaise milega — proposals kaise likhein, low se start karein ya nahi, (6) payments kaise receive karein — PayPal, Payoneer, direct bank transfer, tax implications (GST, ITR), (7) common mistakes jo new freelancers karte hain. Tone practical aur encouraging rakho. Ek realistic income expectation bhi batao pehle 3-6 mahine ke liye.

Write a thoughtful and psychologically-informed guide on how to give a genuine, effective apology — for personal relationships, friendships, and professional settings. Include: (1) what makes an apology genuine vs hollow — the six elements of a meaningful apology (acknowledgment, responsibility, expression of regret, explanation, offer to repair, commitment to change), (2) common apology mistakes that make things worse: 'I'm sorry you feel that way,' 'I'm sorry but…,' over-explaining, (3) how to tailor your apology to the type of hurt caused — minor offense vs serious breach of trust, (4) the difference between apologizing in person, via message, and in writing — when to use which, (5) how to apologize at work professionally without undermining your authority, (6) what to do when your apology is not accepted, (7) self-forgiveness — why it matters and how to practice it. Use real-life example scripts for each scenario. Tone: warm, non-preachy, and psychologically grounded.

Yoga ke physical aur mental fayde par ek informative aur evidence-based Hindi article likho jo beginners ke liye useful ho. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) yoga kya hai — iska history aur different types: Hatha, Ashtanga, Vinyasa, Yin, Restorative, (2) physical fayde: flexibility, strength, posture, breathing capacity, heart health — scientific studies ka reference do, (3) mental fayde: stress reduction, anxiety management, better sleep, improved focus, (4) specific conditions mein yoga ka role — back pain, PCOD, diabetes, hypertension, (5) beginners ke liye 5 easy poses — Tadasana, Balasana, Bhujangasana, Vrikshasana, Shavasana — step-by-step instructions ke saath, (6) yoga kab karein, kitni der karein, aur kya saavedhaniyaan rakhein (pregnancy, injury ke dauran), (7) free YouTube channels aur apps jahan se seekh sakein. Tone positive aur motivating rakho. Yoga ko sirf weight loss se zyada relate karo.

Write a comprehensive, research-backed study skills guide for Indian high school and college students. Address the following areas: (1) why re-reading and highlighting are ineffective — introduce better techniques: active recall, spaced repetition, and the Feynman Technique with a practical example for each, (2) how to create an optimized study environment — lighting, temperature, noise levels, phone placement, (3) how to make effective notes — Cornell method, mind maps, and digital tools like Notion and Obsidian, (4) how to build a sustainable study schedule — time-blocking, the 'study sprint' approach (50 min on, 10 min off), balancing multiple subjects, (5) how to prepare for exams specifically — the difference between 'studying' and 'test preparation,' practice questions, past papers, (6) how to manage exam anxiety with breathing and grounding techniques, (7) how sleep, nutrition, and exercise directly affect memory consolidation. Include a one-week sample study timetable for board exam preparation. Tone: practical and evidence-based, avoiding generic advice.

Personal relationships — dosti ho, family ho, ya romantic relationship — mein trust build karne aur maintain karne ke baare mein ek deep aur practical Hindi guide likho. Niche diye topics include karo: (1) trust kya hota hai aur yeh kyun kisi bhi relationship ki neenv hai, (2) trust banane mein kitna waqt lagta hai aur kitni jaldi toot sakta hai — research se examples, (3) practical behaviors jo trust badhate hain: consistency, honesty, vulnerability, reliability, (4) kaise pata karein ki koi aap par trust karta hai ya nahi, (5) tuta hua trust dobara kaise build karein — step by step process forgiveness aur rebuilding ke baad, (6) trust aur control mein fark karna — jealousy, possessiveness ko trust samajhna galat kyun hai, (7) jab baar baar trust toota ho to therapy ya counseling kab leni chahiye. Tone compassionate aur balanced rakho. Real-life scenarios use karo har point ko illustrate karne ke liye.

Write a motivating and practical guide for someone who wants to develop a reading habit but has struggled to stick with it or doesn't know where to start. Include: (1) reframe the common excuses — 'I don't have time,' 'I can't concentrate,' 'Books bore me' — with honest solutions for each, (2) how to choose your first book — start with what genuinely interests you, not what seems impressive; include a genre quiz framework, (3) practical strategies to build the habit: habit stacking (read after a fixed daily activity), keeping a book visible, starting with just 10 pages a day, (4) the difference between physical books, e-books, and audiobooks — pros, cons, and best use cases, (5) how to read actively — annotating, asking questions while reading, keeping a reading journal, (6) how to track progress and stay motivated — Goodreads, reading challenges, book clubs, (7) a curated starter list of 10 highly readable books across genres (fiction, non-fiction, self-help, Indian writing). Tone: enthusiastic, inclusive, and pressure-free.

input: paise ki bachat kaise karein ghar mein
target: Ek middle-class Indian family ke liye ghar ke kharcho ko kam karne aur savings badhane ki practical guide Hindi mein likho. Yeh un logon ke liye hai jinki monthly income ₹30,000–₹80,000 ke beech hai. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) pehle apna monthly budget banao — income vs fixed expenses vs variable expenses ka breakdown, (2) ghar ka ration aur grocery smarter kaise khareedein — bulk buying, seasonal fruits/vegetables, brands ke alternatives, (3) bijli ka bill kaise kam karein — energy-efficient appliances, habits jo bill badhate hain, (4) EMI aur loan ko manage kaise karein — high-interest credit card debt pehle khatam karo, (5) unnecessary subscriptions aur spending patterns identify karna — 30-day rule, (6) bachon ki padhai ke liye aage se planning — Sukanya Samriddhi, PPF, (7) ghar mein doosri income kaise create karein — tuition, tiffin service, online selling. Tone practical aur respectful rakho. Family ke emotions ko bhi acknowledge karo.

Explain blockchain technology to a non-technical reader who has heard the term but has no idea what it actually means. Structure the explanation as follows: (1) start with a simple, memorable analogy — describe blockchain as a shared Google Doc that thousands of people can see but nobody can secretly edit, (2) explain the core concept: distributed ledger, nodes, and why no single person controls it, (3) walk through how a single Bitcoin transaction works step by step — from initiation to confirmation — using plain language, (4) explain what 'mining' is and why it requires so much energy, (5) differentiate between blockchain and cryptocurrency — blockchain is the technology, crypto is one application, (6) describe three real-world uses beyond crypto: supply chain tracking, digital voting, land records (mention India's pilot programs), (7) address common misconceptions: blockchain is not anonymous, it's not unhackable, it's not only for finance. Use diagrams described in text and a FAQ section at the end. Avoid technical jargon without explanation.

Social anxiety — logon ke saamne baat karne ya public situations mein ghabrahat — se deal karne ke baare mein ek empathetic aur practical Hindi guide likho. Yeh un logon ke liye hai jo social situations mein bahut nervous ya uncomfortable feel karte hain. Cover karo: (1) social anxiety kya hoti hai — shyness se kaise alag hai, clinical disorder kab banta hai, (2) common symptoms: dhadkan teez hona, paseena aana, thoughts race karna, khud ko embarrass hone ka dar, (3) roots — childhood experiences, perfectionism, negative self-talk kaise contribute karte hain, (4) Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) ke simple techniques jo ghar par apply ho sakein — thought challenging, behavioral experiments, (5) exposure therapy ka concept — dheere dheere uncomfortable situations face karna, (6) practical daily tips: conversation starters, eye contact, group situations mein kaise behave karein, (7) kab professional help zaroor leni chahiye — therapist ya psychiatrist. Tone safe, validating, aur practical rakho. Kisi ko bhi 'overreact' karne ka feel mat karao.

Write a practical, science-backed guide to becoming an early riser for someone who currently struggles to wake up before 8 AM. Include: (1) the science of circadian rhythm — why your body resists early wake-ups and how to gradually shift it, (2) the gradual shift method: move your wake-up time 15 minutes earlier every 2–3 days rather than jolting to 5 AM overnight, (3) how to make the night before count — ideal bedtime, screen cutoff, room temperature, alarm placement, (4) how to win the first 5 minutes after waking — defeating the snooze button with identity-based habit tricks, (5) what to do in the first hour that makes early rising feel rewarding rather than punishing (not scrolling phones), (6) how weekends disrupt the system and what to do about 'social jet lag,' (7) realistic expectations — not everyone's chronotype suits 5 AM, and that's okay. Include a 2-week transition schedule. Tone: realistic and non-shaming, acknowledging that it genuinely is hard.

Ek beginner ke liye basic everyday makeup guide Hindi mein likho jo pehli baar makeup try karna chahti ho. Guide mein yeh sab shamil karo: (1) pehle apni skin type aur undertone samjho — yeh kyun zaroori hai products choose karte waqt, (2) essential products jo shuruwaat mein lene chahiye — sunscreen, moisturizer, foundation ya BB cream, kajal, mascara, lip color — India mein available aur affordable brands ke saath (Lakme, Maybelline, Faces Canada), (3) skin preparation kaise karein — cleanse, tone, moisturize, SPF, (4) step-by-step daytime natural look tutorial: primer, foundation, concealer, blush, eyebrows, simple eye makeup, lips, (5) beginner mistakes: galat shade choose karna, blending na karna, kya avoid karein, (6) makeup remove karna kitna zaroori hai aur kaise karein, (7) kis tools ki zaroorat hai — brushes vs fingers vs sponge. Tone encouraging aur non-intimidating rakho. Yeh batao ki makeup sikhne mein waqt lagta hai aur practice se aata hai.

input: how to deal with a toxic friend
target: Write a compassionate, psychologically grounded guide on how to recognize and handle a toxic friendship for a young adult audience. Include: (1) clear signs that a friendship has become toxic — one-sided effort, constant criticism, jealousy of your success, guilt-tripping, emotional manipulation — with realistic examples, (2) how to distinguish between a friend going through a hard time versus a genuinely toxic pattern, (3) the psychological cost of staying in toxic friendships — on self-esteem, anxiety, and life decisions, (4) how to set limits within the friendship without immediately ending it — using 'I feel' statements, reducing availability, stopping certain topics, (5) how to have 'the conversation' if you decide to address the issue directly — what to say and what to avoid, (6) how to end a friendship gracefully — gradual fade vs direct conversation, what to do if they react badly, (7) how to deal with the grief and guilt that comes after ending a long friendship. Tone: empathetic, non-judgmental, and validating. Acknowledge that ending friendships is emotionally hard.

Job interview ke liye kapde choose karne ke baare mein ek practical aur industry-specific Hindi guide likho. Yeh guide un Indian job seekers ke liye hai jo confuse hain ki kya pehna jaye. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) 'dress for the job' concept kya hota hai — company culture aur industry ke hisaab se dressing kaise adjust karein, (2) IT/tech companies ke liye — smart casual kya hota hai, jeans aur chinos ka acceptable use, kya avoid karein, (3) banking, finance, consulting ke liye — formal dress code: suit, blazer, tie kab zaroori hai, (4) creative industries (advertising, media, design) ke liye — yahan overdressed hona bhi galat ho sakta hai, (5) government jobs aur PSU interviews ke liye — conservative, formal look, (6) women ke liye industry-wise suggestions — saree, salwar, western formal sab ke pros/cons, (7) grooming details jo log ignore karte hain: nails, hair, fragrance, clean shoes, ironed clothes. Tone practical aur respectful rakho. Budget-friendly options bhi dena.

Write a clear, honest explainer on cryptocurrency for an Indian reader who has heard about it from friends or social media but doesn't understand what it is. Structure it as follows: (1) start with the fundamental problem cryptocurrency tries to solve — trust in financial transactions without a middleman like a bank, (2) explain what Bitcoin is and how it works at a basic level — miners, transactions, finite supply of 21 million coins, (3) explain the difference between Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies (Ethereum, Ripple, Dogecoin) — what makes each different, (4) explain how to buy, store, and sell crypto in India — exchanges like WazirX, CoinDCX, crypto wallets (hot vs cold), (5) explain the current legal status of crypto in India — RBI's stance, 30% flat tax on gains, TDS provisions, (6) lay out the real risks honestly: extreme volatility, scams, rug pulls, exchange failures, regulatory uncertainty, (7) distinguish between legitimate investing and speculative gambling in crypto — what questions to ask before putting in money. Tone: informative and honest, neither hyping crypto nor dismissing it. No financial advice — just education.

input: bache ko study mein kaise help karein
target: Parents ke liye ek comprehensive guide Hindi mein likho jo apne bacche (class 3–10) ki padhai mein ghar par effectively help karna chahte hain. Niche diye topics include karo: (1) bacche ka homework ya study session kaise set up karein — distraction-free space, time, routine, (2) bacche ki study style samjhna — visual, auditory, kinesthetic learners ke liye alag approaches, (3) padhne mein help karna bina unka kaam khud kiye — questions poochho, explain karwao, hints do, (4) maths mein weak bacchon ki help kaise karein — rote learning ki jagah concept-based understanding, (5) bacche ki school se baat karein — teacher ke saath effective communication kaise karein, (6) kab tuition lena chahiye aur kaise sahi tutor choose karein, (7) bacche ka motivation aur confidence kaise badhayein bina pressure diye — praise kaise dein, comparison se kaise bachein. Tone warm aur supportive rakho — parents ki mehnat ko acknowledge karo.

input: how to make friends as an adult
target: Write an honest, practical guide on making meaningful friendships as an adult (25–40 years old), acknowledging that it genuinely gets harder after college. Include: (1) why making friends as an adult is objectively harder — less proximity, less repetition, less spontaneity than school/college — validate this struggle, (2) the science of friendship: the three key ingredients — proximity, repeated unplanned interaction, a setting that encourages vulnerability, (3) where to actually meet people as an adult in India: hobby classes, running clubs, book clubs, volunteer groups, language exchange meetups, professional networking events, online communities, (4) how to move from 'acquaintance' to 'actual friend' — the initiating step most people skip: asking to hang out one-on-one, (5) how to be a good friend — showing up, remembering details, reaching out first, being present, (6) what to do about shyness or social anxiety when trying to meet new people, (7) how to maintain friendships when life gets busy — scheduling intentional time, digital check-ins. Tone: warm, honest, and encouraging. Acknowledge loneliness without making it heavier.

Hair fall ki samasya se pareshan logon ke liye ek comprehensive aur evidence-based Hindi guide likho. Yeh guide un logon ke liye hai jo zyada baal jhadne se pareshan hain aur ghar par aur medically dono taraf se solutions dhundhna chahte hain. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) normal vs abnormal hair fall kya hota hai — rozaana kitne baal jhadna theek hai, (2) common causes: nutrition deficiency (iron, vitamin D, biotin), thyroid, stress (telogen effluvium), hormonal changes, dandruff, wrong hair care habits, (3) diet mein kya badlav karein — baalon ke liye best foods, kya avoid karein, (4) sahi hair care routine — shampoo frequency, conditioner, oiling — sach kya hai aur myths kya hain, (5) home remedies jo actually kaam karti hain (onion juice, scalp massage) vs jo sirf viral hain bina proof ke, (6) kab dermatologist ke paas jaana chahiye aur kaunsi tests karwani chahiye (blood tests), (7) medical treatments — Minoxidil, PRP — kya hain aur kab consider karein. Tone informative aur realistic rakho. False promises mat do.

Write a psychologically grounded, practical guide on building genuine self-confidence for young adults who feel chronically insecure or self-doubting. Avoid generic motivational advice. Include: (1) distinguish between true confidence and performative confidence — why 'fake it till you make it' has limits, (2) the cognitive roots of low confidence: negative core beliefs, harsh inner critic, cognitive distortions like catastrophizing and mind-reading, (3) how to challenge your inner critic with Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) techniques — thought records, evidence gathering, (4) the role of competence in confidence — confidence follows action, not the other way around — give the 'confidence ladder' framework, (5) body language and its bidirectional effect on confidence — posture, voice, eye contact — backed by social psychology research, (6) how to stop seeking external validation and build intrinsic self-worth through values-based living, (7) daily micro-practices: doing one uncomfortable thing daily, keeping a 'wins journal,' setting and completing small goals. Tone: honest, warm, and grounded — not toxic positivity.

input: desh se bahar job kaise milegi
target: India se bahar (USA, Canada, UK, Germany, Australia, Dubai) mein job dhundne ke baare mein ek step-by-step practical Hindi guide likho. Yeh guide un logon ke liye hai jo India mein kaam kar rahe hain aur abroad job opportunity dhundna chahte hain. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) pehle decide karo — kaunsa desh, kyon, aur wahan settle karna hai ya sirf kaam karna? Har desh ke pros/cons, (2) aapki current skills aur qualifications internationally kitni valuable hain — gap analysis kaise karein, (3) resume/CV ko international format mein kaise convert karein — country-specific differences, (4) kahan dhundhen — LinkedIn, Indeed, Glassdoor, country-specific job portals, company websites direct apply, (5) work visa ke types har desh ke liye ek brief overview — H1-B, Skilled Worker Visa, PR pathways, (6) interview process internationally kaise alag hoti hai — phone screening, panel interviews, technical rounds, (7) relocation cost, cost of living, aur salary expectations realistic rakhna. Tone practical aur informed rakho. False hopes mat do — yeh process long aur uncertain hai.

input: how to talk to someone who is depressed
target: Write a sensitive, evidence-informed guide for someone who wants to support a friend, partner, or family member who is going through depression but doesn't know what to say or do. Include: (1) what depression feels like from the inside — help the reader develop empathy before they speak, (2) what to say vs what not to say — include specific example phrases with explanations (e.g., don't say 'just be positive,' do say 'I'm here with you'), (3) how to start the conversation — choosing the right moment and setting, how to bring it up without it feeling like a confrontation, (4) the role of active listening — what it looks like in practice: not fixing, not comparing, just being present, (5) practical ways to help beyond words: showing up with food, going for a walk together, accompanying them to a doctor, (6) how to encourage professional help without pressure or ultimatums, (7) how to take care of yourself as the support person — caregiver burnout is real, and you matter too. Include a 'what to avoid' checklist. Tone: gentle, honest, and compassionate.

Car driving sikhne wale beginners ke liye ek comprehensive aur practical Hindi guide likho. Guide mein yeh sab include karo: (1) license lene se pehle kya karna chahiye — learner's license kaise apply karein, documents kya chahiye, RTO process, (2) car ke basic controls samjho — accelerator, brake, clutch (manual transmission), steering, gears, handbrake — ek ek ka kaam, (3) manual vs automatic car mein se beginners ke liye kaunsi zyada aasaan hai aur kyun, (4) pehli baar steering seat par baithne se pehle — seat, mirrors, seat belt — sahi position kaise set karein, (5) chalane ki shuruwaat kaise karein — clutch-accelerator coordination (manual) ya simple D/R (automatic) — slow area mein practice, (6) common beginner mistakes: gahabarana, wrong gear, sudden braking, lane sense na hona — kaise bachein, (7) India ki roads aur traffic conditions ke liye specific tips — aggressive drivers, auto/cycle/pedestrians, bad roads. Tone patient aur reassuring rakho. Yeh batao ki driving practice se hi aati hai.

Write a practical, evidence-based guide to eating healthily for an average Indian adult who wants to improve their diet without following extreme diets or giving up Indian food. Include: (1) debunk the most common diet myths circulating in India — detox teas, cutting all carbs, fruit-only diets, skipping meals, (2) explain the basics of balanced nutrition in the Indian context: carbohydrates (roti/rice), protein (dal, paneer, eggs, chicken), healthy fats (ghee, nuts, seeds), and fibre (vegetables, fruits), (3) how to make small, sustainable swaps in typical Indian meals — e.g., white rice to brown rice or millets, maida to atta, fried snacks to roasted alternatives, (4) the importance of portion size vs food quality — you can overeat 'healthy' food too, (5) how to build a balanced plate: the 'thali method' — half vegetables, quarter protein, quarter carbs, (6) hydration, meal timing, and why skipping breakfast is often counterproductive, (7) realistic tips for eating healthy on a budget and when eating at restaurants or ordering in. Tone: non-restrictive, culturally sensitive, and sustainable.

Public speaking aur achi speech dene ke baare mein ek step-by-step Hindi guide likho un logon ke liye jo stage par nervous ho jaate hain ya pehli baar kisi event mein speak karne wale hain. Niche diye topics include karo: (1) public speaking ka dar — glossophobia — kyon hota hai aur yeh normal kyun hai, (2) speech ki taiyari: topic research, main points decide karna, structure banana — introduction, body, conclusion, (3) opening line powerful kaise banayein — hook kya hota hai, interesting story/question/fact se shuruwaat, (4) body language: stage par khade hone ka sahi tarika, haath kaise use karein, eye contact maintain karna, (5) awaaz: clearly bolna, speed control, pause ka sahi use, volume, (6) nervousness ko manage karna during the speech — breathing techniques, audience ko friendly samajhna, (7) practice methods: mirror practice, recording, audience ke saamne rehearsal, Toastmasters jaise clubs. Tone encouraging aur step-by-step rakho. Batao ki har bade speaker bhi pehle nervous tha.

Write a clear, engaging explanation of inflation for a general reader in India who wants to understand why prices keep rising but has no economics background. Structure the explanation as: (1) start with a relatable example — the price of chai, petrol, or a movie ticket 10 years ago vs today, (2) define inflation in plain terms and introduce the CPI (Consumer Price Index) as the measurement tool, (3) explain the three main types and causes: demand-pull inflation (too much money chasing too few goods), cost-push inflation (input costs rising — oil, raw materials), and built-in inflation (wage-price spiral), (4) explain how RBI's monetary policy and interest rates are used to control inflation — the repo rate mechanism simply, (5) how inflation in India is affected by monsoon and food prices specifically — the Indian context, (6) who is most hurt by inflation and who benefits — fixed income earners vs asset holders, borrowers vs lenders, (7) what individuals can do to protect their savings from inflation — real returns, equity investing, gold. Use simple analogies throughout. No jargon without explanation.

Internet se legitimate tarikon se paisa kamane ke baare mein ek honest aur practical Hindi guide likho. Yeh guide students, housewives, aur anyone ke liye hai jo ghar se ya part-time additional income chahte hain. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) clearly batao ki 'get rich quick' schemes aur genuine online income mein fark hai — scams se kaise bachein, (2) skill-based options: freelance writing, graphic design, web development, video editing — kahan seekhein, kahan kaam milega, realistic earnings, (3) content creation: YouTube, Instagram Reels, blogging — kitna waqt lagta hai monetize hone mein, realistic expectations, (4) online tutoring aur teaching — Vedantu, Unacademy jaise platforms par ya independently, (5) e-commerce: Amazon, Meesho, Etsy par selling — products kya bechein, logistics, startup cost, (6) micro-tasks aur surveys — Amazon MTurk, Swagbucks — low paying but easy for beginners, (7) passive income options — stock photos, digital products, print-on-demand. Tax implications bhi briefly mention karo. Tone honest rakho — yeh koi jaldi amir hone ka zariya nahi hai.

Write a psychologically-informed, practical guide on overcoming procrastination for people who know they need to stop but can't seem to. Avoid generic advice like 'just start.' Include: (1) what procrastination actually is — not laziness but an emotion regulation problem (avoiding anxiety, boredom, self-doubt connected to a task), backed by psychological research, (2) the four types of procrastinators: the perfectionist, the overwhelmed, the self-saboteur, and the thrill-seeker — help readers identify themselves, (3) the 'two-minute rule' and 'implementation intentions' (if-then planning) as evidence-based starter techniques, (4) how to make hard tasks less emotionally threatening: breaking them into 'stupid small' steps, time-boxing, lowering the stakes with a first draft mindset, (5) environmental design — removing friction and temptation: phone in another room, website blockers, dedicated workspace, (6) self-compassion as a tool — why being harsh on yourself for procrastinating makes it worse, not better, (7) how to build momentum once started and avoid re-procrastination. Include a practical 'anti-procrastination protocol' checklist. Tone: understanding, direct, and action-oriented.

input: India mein ghar khareedne ki guide
target: Pehli baar ghar khareedne wale Indian middle-class buyers ke liye ek comprehensive step-by-step Hindi guide likho. Niche diye topics include karo: (1) ready-to-move vs under-construction property — dono ke fayde aur nuksaan, (2) location choose karne ke factors: connectivity, schools, hospitals, future development plans, resale value, (3) builder ya developer ka background check kaise karein — RERA registration verify karna, past projects dekho, reviews check karo, (4) home loan process: eligibility, documents, comparison between banks vs HFCs, interest types (fixed vs floating), (5) hidden costs jo log bhool jaate hain — registration charges, stamp duty, GST, society maintenance, interior, brokerage, (6) legal due diligence: title deed, encumbrance certificate, building plan approval, NOC kya hote hain, (7) property purchase ke baad kya karein — registration, mutation, society membership. Tone practical aur calm rakho — yeh ek bada financial decision hai, uski seriousness acknowledge karo bina daraye.

Write a warm, insightful guide on building and maintaining healthy romantic relationships for young adults in India (20–35). Base the advice on relationship psychology research, not just generic feel-good advice. Cover: (1) what genuinely healthy relationships look like — not perfect, but safe, honest, and mutual — contrasted with the romanticized toxic relationship tropes in Indian cinema, (2) the four horsemen of relationship destruction (John Gottman's research): contempt, criticism, defensiveness, stonewalling — with Indian-context examples, (3) how to communicate about needs without it becoming a fight — using 'I' statements, choosing the right moment, not attacking character, (4) the importance of maintaining individual identity within a relationship — common interests vs personal space and friendships, (5) how to handle disagreements about money, family interference (especially in-laws), and future plans — practical frameworks, (6) how to rebuild trust after a breach — steps, timeline, what realistic repair looks like, (7) green flags to look for in a partner that predict long-term relationship health. Tone: honest, research-grounded, and respectful of diverse relationship styles.

input: photography sikhni hai beginners ke liye
target: Photography mein interest rakhne wale beginners ke liye ek complete starter guide Hindi mein likho. Yeh guide un logon ke liye hai jinhone camera ya phone se photos lena shuru karna hai. Cover karo: (1) kaunse camera se shuru karein — DSLR, mirrorless, ya smartphone? Budget ke hisaab se comparison, (2) teen sabse zaroori camera settings jo samajhne chahiye: aperture (f-number), shutter speed, aur ISO — 'exposure triangle' simple bhasha mein, (3) composition rules jo immediately better photos dilate hain — rule of thirds, leading lines, framing, negative space, (4) natural light ka use kaise karein — 'golden hour,' harsh midday light se bachna, window light indoors, (5) smartphone photography tips — portrait mode, grid lines, manual mode apps jaise ProCam ya Open Camera, (6) photos edit kaise karein — free tools: Snapseed, Lightroom Mobile — basic adjustments: exposure, contrast, color grading, (7) portfolio kaise banayein aur Instagram par apna photography account kaise grow karein. Tone enthusiastic aur encouraging rakho. Expensive gear ki zaroorat nahi hoti — yeh baat emphasize karo.

Write a clear, accessible introduction to the Indian Constitution for a general reader — not a law student — who wants to understand what it actually is and what it means for them. Structure it as follows: (1) what is a constitution and why India needed one after independence — brief historical context, (2) who wrote it — the Constituent Assembly, Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's role, and how long it took (2 years, 11 months, 18 days), (3) the Preamble — explain each key word: Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic, Justice, Liberty, Equality, Fraternity — in plain language, (4) the three branches of government and how they are defined by the Constitution: Legislature (Parliament), Executive (President, PM, Cabinet), Judiciary (Supreme Court), (5) Fundamental Rights — list and briefly explain the six categories and why they matter to ordinary citizens, (6) Fundamental Duties — what citizens owe in return, (7) how the Constitution can be amended — why this matters for democracy. Include a FAQ section at the end. Tone: engaging, non-partisan, and civics-focused.

input: weight loss ke liye kya karein
target: Weight loss ke baare mein ek honest, evidence-based aur sustainable Hindi guide likho jo crash diets ya unrealistic results promote na kare. Yeh guide un logon ke liye hai jo healthy weight pe aana chahte hain, especially Indians jo Indian food chhod nahi sakte. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) weight loss ka asli science: calorie deficit kya hota hai, aur yeh kyun zaroori hai — simple equation, (2) Indian diet mein changes: kya kam karein, kya badhayein — specific foods ke saath, (3) exercise ka role: sirf cardio enough nahi — strength training kyun zaroori hai fat loss ke liye, (4) crash diets, GM diet, juice fasts kyon kaam nahi karte long term mein — yo-yo effect samjhao, (5) sleep aur stress ka weight par seedha asar — cortisol aur ghrelin hormones briefly, (6) realistic expectations: healthy weight loss ki speed kya hoti hai — 0.5–1 kg per week, (7) kab doctor ya dietitian se milna chahiye — thyroid, PCOD jaise conditions mein medical supervision kyun zaroori hai. Tone non-judgmental aur body-positive rakho. Kisi ko fat-shame mat karo.

Write a practical, modern guide on professional networking for Indian working adults and recent graduates who find networking awkward or transactional. Include: (1) reframe what networking actually is — not schmoozing or collecting contacts, but building genuine mutual professional relationships over time, (2) where to network in India: LinkedIn, industry conferences, alumni networks, professional associations, Twitter/X communities, and local startup events, (3) how to optimize your LinkedIn profile to make people want to connect — headline, about section, featured posts, recommendations, (4) how to reach out to someone you don't know — cold message/email framework that feels human, not spammy, with an example template, (5) how to nurture existing network connections — congratulating milestones, sharing useful content, coffee chat requests, (6) how to network at events — starting conversations, graceful exits, following up within 24 hours, (7) for introverts: how to make networking feel less draining — one meaningful conversation vs 20 shallow ones. Tone: practical, non-awkward, and ethical. Emphasize giving value before asking for favors.

Anxiety — ghabrahat aur bechain rehna — ko manage karne ke baare mein ek thorough, compassionate aur practical Hindi guide likho jo common logon ke kaam aaye. Yeh clinical advice nahi hai, lekin evidence-based hai. Cover karo: (1) anxiety kya hai — iska biological reason: amygdala, fight-or-flight response — simple bhasha mein, (2) normal anxiety vs anxiety disorder mein fark kab hota hai, (3) physical symptoms: dhadkan, paseena, chakkar, tight chest — yeh sab brain-body connection hai samjhao, (4) turant rahat ke liye techniques: box breathing (4-4-4-4), cold water on face, grounding technique (5 cheezein dekho, 4 chhuye, etc.), (5) long-term anxiety kam karne ke liye lifestyle changes: regular exercise, caffeine kam karna, consistent sleep, journaling, (6) cognitive techniques: anxious thoughts ko challenge karna — 'Is yeh thought fact hai ya feeling?' framework, (7) kab psychiatrist ya therapist se milna zaroor hai — aur India mein yeh help kaise lein (online therapy options bhi). Tone validating aur calm rakho — anxiety feel karna unki galti nahi hai.

Create a structured 12-week Python learning roadmap for a complete beginner with no prior programming experience. Include: (1) why Python is a good first language — readability, job market demand, versatility, (2) how to set up your environment — installing Python 3, VS Code, and configuring your first project folder, (3) week-by-week curriculum: variables and data types (weeks 1–2), control flow and loops (weeks 3–4), functions and scope (weeks 5–6), lists/dictionaries/tuples (weeks 7–8), file handling and exceptions (weeks 9–10), OOP basics (weeks 11–12), (4) best free resources: Python.org docs, freeCodeCamp YouTube, CS50P, Automate the Boring Stuff, (5) the importance of building projects from week 1 — suggest 3 beginner projects: a calculator, a to-do list app, a simple number guessing game, (6) common beginner mistakes: not practicing daily, copying code without understanding, skipping error messages. Tone: encouraging and structured. Format as a weekly planner with clear milestones.

Web development mein naye logon ke liye HTML aur CSS seekhne ka ek complete beginner guide Hindi mein banao. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) HTML kya hai — webpage ki structure kaise banti hai, tags, elements, aur attributes ka basic concept, (2) zaroori HTML tags: html, head, body, h1-h6, p, a, img, ul/ol, div, span — har ek ka use case example ke saath, (3) CSS kya karta hai — styling kaise apply hoti hai, selectors (class, id, element) ka fark, (4) CSS box model samjhao — margin, padding, border, content, (5) Flexbox basics — ek simple navigation bar aur card layout banao step by step, (6) responsive design kya hota hai aur media queries ka basic use, (7) pehla project: ek simple personal portfolio webpage banao jisme photo, bio, aur contact section ho. Free tools: VS Code, CodePen, MDN Web Docs. Tone beginner-friendly aur practical rakho.

Write a clear, in-depth explanation of recursion for a beginner programmer who understands basic functions but finds recursion confusing. Structure it as follows: (1) define recursion in plain English — a function that calls itself — and explain why it exists (problems that have self-similar substructure), (2) use the classic factorial example to show the base case and recursive case step by step, tracing the call stack visually with indentation, (3) explain the call stack — what happens in memory when recursive calls pile up, and what a stack overflow is, (4) walk through a second example: Fibonacci sequence — show both the naive recursive approach and why it's inefficient (exponential time), (5) introduce memoization as a fix for redundant recursive calls — show code before and after, (6) explain when to use recursion vs iteration — tree traversal, directory scanning, divide-and-conquer algorithms as ideal use cases, (7) provide three practice problems of increasing difficulty with hints. Use Python code examples throughout. Tone: patient, visual, and thorough.

JavaScript arrays ko beginners ke liye ek comprehensive Hindi tutorial mein explain karo. Niche diye topics include karo: (1) array kya hota hai — ek hi variable mein multiple values store karna, real-life analogy ke saath (jaise shopping list), (2) array declare karne ke tarike — literal syntax vs Array constructor, (3) indexing — 0-based indexing kya hoti hai, elements access karna aur modify karna, (4) zaroori array methods detail mein: push(), pop(), shift(), unshift(), splice(), slice() — har ek ke liye code example aur output, (5) iteration methods: forEach(), map(), filter(), reduce() — kab kaunsa use karein, real use cases ke saath, (6) 2D arrays — nested arrays kya hote hain, kaise access karein, (7) common beginner mistakes: mutation vs copying (spread operator ka use), length property ka sahi use, undefined vs out-of-bounds. Har concept ke baad ek chhota practice exercise bhi do. Code snippets browser console mein run ho sakein.

Write a comprehensive yet beginner-accessible explanation of REST APIs for someone who has learned basic web development but has never worked with APIs. Include: (1) start with an analogy — a restaurant menu and waiter system — to explain client, server, request, and response, (2) define API and REST: what REST stands for, the six architectural constraints briefly, (3) HTTP methods in detail: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE — what each is used for with real-world examples (fetching a user profile, creating a post, updating a name, deleting an account), (4) HTTP status codes every developer should know: 200, 201, 400, 401, 403, 404, 500 — with simple descriptions, (5) what JSON is and why it's the standard data format for REST APIs — show a sample JSON response, (6) how to make API calls in JavaScript using the Fetch API and async/await — a working code example hitting a free public API (e.g., JSONPlaceholder or OpenWeatherMap), (7) how to test APIs using Postman — a step-by-step guide for a GET and POST request. Tone: practical and project-oriented.

input: Git aur GitHub kya hota hai
target: Git aur GitHub ko pehli baar seekhne wale beginners ke liye ek step-by-step Hindi guide banao. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) version control kya hota hai — bina Git ke kaam karne ki problems: 'final_final_v3.docx' wali situation se analogy, (2) Git vs GitHub mein fark — Git ek local tool hai, GitHub ek cloud platform, (3) Git install karna aur initial setup: git config name aur email, (4) core Git workflow: git init, git add, git commit — ek simple project par demonstrate karo, (5) git status, git log, git diff — kya kaam aate hain, (6) branching concept: branch kyon banate hain, git branch, git checkout, git merge — ek feature branch workflow example ke saath, (7) GitHub par kaam: repository create karna, git remote add, git push, git pull, aur pull request kya hoti hai. Common mistakes: .gitignore ka importance, sensitive data accidentally push karna — kaise bachein. Tone friendly aur sequential rakho.

Write a thorough introduction to Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) for a beginner who knows basic procedural programming in Python or Java. Cover: (1) why OOP exists — the problem with large procedural codebases, the need to model real-world entities in code, (2) the four pillars of OOP with clear explanations and code examples for each: Encapsulation (bundling data and methods, using private attributes), Abstraction (hiding complexity behind simple interfaces), Inheritance (child classes reusing parent class behavior — show a Vehicle → Car → ElectricCar hierarchy), Polymorphism (same method name, different behavior — method overriding example), (3) classes and objects: the difference between a blueprint and an instance, __init__ constructor, self keyword, (4) dunder/magic methods: __str__, __repr__, __len__ — when and why to use them, (5) composition vs inheritance — when to prefer one over the other with a real-world design scenario, (6) a practical mini-project: design a simple Library Management System using OOP principles, (7) common OOP mistakes beginners make. Use Python throughout. Format as a tutorial with code blocks and outputs.

input: SQL basics sikhao database ke liye
target: Database aur SQL seekhne wale complete beginners ke liye ek comprehensive Hindi tutorial likho. Niche diye topics include karo: (1) database kya hota hai — spreadsheet se compare karke samjhao, relational database ka concept, (2) SQL kya hai aur kahan use hota hai — MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite mein fark briefly, (3) basic DDL commands: CREATE TABLE, ALTER TABLE, DROP TABLE — data types ke saath (INT, VARCHAR, DATE, BOOLEAN), (4) basic DML commands: INSERT INTO, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE — WHERE clause ka zaroori role, (5) SELECT ke advanced use: ORDER BY, GROUP BY, HAVING, LIMIT — real examples ke saath, (6) JOINs explain karo — INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN — ek students aur courses table ka example use karke visually samjhao, (7) ek chhota practical project: ek school ka database design karo jisme students, teachers, aur classes tables hon aur 5 useful queries likhein. Free tools: DB Browser for SQLite, MySQL Workbench. Tone structured aur example-heavy rakho.

Write a practical, comprehensive guide on debugging code for beginner-to-intermediate developers who feel lost when their code breaks. Include: (1) the mindset shift — debugging is a skill, not a sign of failure; even senior engineers spend 50% of their time debugging, (2) the scientific method applied to debugging: observe the bug, form a hypothesis, test it, revise — walk through a concrete example, (3) reading error messages effectively — stack traces, what each line means, how to find the line that actually caused the error vs where it was reported, (4) using print/console.log debugging effectively — placement strategy, logging variables at key checkpoints, (5) using an IDE debugger: breakpoints, step-over, step-into, watch variables — demonstrated in VS Code with Python or JavaScript, (6) rubber duck debugging and the question-asking framework — how to explain your code to yourself or others to find the bug, (7) using browser DevTools for frontend bugs — console, network tab, elements inspector, (8) how to write a good Stack Overflow question when you're truly stuck. Tone: practical, reassuring, and methodical.

input: React JS kya hai kaise seekhein
target: React JS ko beginners ke liye ek structured Hindi learning guide mein explain karo. Yeh guide un logon ke liye hai jinhein basic HTML, CSS, aur JavaScript aati hai. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) React kya hai aur kyun popular hai — traditional DOM manipulation se kya problem thi, Virtual DOM ka concept simply, (2) React environment setup: Node.js install karna, create-react-app ya Vite se project banana, folder structure samjhana, (3) JSX kya hota hai — JavaScript mein HTML likhna, basic rules aur common syntax errors, (4) Components: functional components kya hote hain, props kaise pass karte hain, components ko reuse karne ka faida, (5) State aur useState hook — state kya hoti hai, useState ka syntax, ek counter app example step by step, (6) useEffect hook — side effects kya hote hain, API call kaise karte hain useEffect se, dependency array ka role, (7) ek complete beginner project: ek simple Todo App banao jisme add, delete, aur complete functionality ho. Common mistakes beginners karte hain. Free resources: official React docs (react.dev), Scrimba React course.

Write a clear, engaging explanation of Big O notation for a self-taught developer or CS student who has heard the term but doesn't fully understand it. Structure it as follows: (1) why Big O matters — when does algorithm efficiency actually become a problem? Give a real-world example: searching through 100 records vs 100 million records, (2) define Big O in plain English: it describes how the runtime or space requirements of an algorithm grow as input size grows — not the exact time, but the growth rate, (3) walk through the most common complexities with a simple code example and graph description for each: O(1) constant, O(log n) binary search, O(n) linear search, O(n log n) merge sort, O(n²) bubble sort, O(2ⁿ) recursive Fibonacci, (4) how to calculate Big O for a given piece of code — rules: drop constants, drop non-dominant terms, (5) space complexity vs time complexity — what each measures and why both matter, (6) why O(n log n) is often the best achievable for comparison-based sorting (briefly), (7) practical exercises: give 5 code snippets and ask readers to determine Big O with answers. Tone: visual, methodical, and approachable.

Python mein files ke saath kaam karna seekhne wale beginners ke liye ek detailed Hindi tutorial likho. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) file handling kyon zaroori hai — real programs ko data save aur load karna padta hai, database ka alternative simple cases mein, (2) files open karna: open() function ka syntax, file modes — 'r', 'w', 'a', 'rb', 'wb' — har ek ka kab use karein, (3) with statement kyon use karna chahiye — context manager kya hai, file automatically close karna, memory leaks se bachna, (4) text files: read(), readline(), readlines() ka fark — kab kaunsa use karein, ek log file padhne ka example, (5) files mein likhna: write() aur writelines() — ek simple student records file create karo, (6) CSV files handle karna: Python ka built-in csv module — reader aur writer objects, ek practical example: student marks CSV padhna aur nayi row add karna, (7) JSON files: json.load() aur json.dump() — API response save karna aur dobara load karna. Error handling: FileNotFoundError, PermissionError kaise handle karein. Practice exercises bhi do.

Write a thorough yet accessible explanation of how the internet works for a beginner programmer or curious non-technical person. Structure it as follows: (1) the physical infrastructure — underwater cables, data centers, ISPs, and the last-mile connection to your home, explained simply, (2) IP addresses and DNS — what an IP address is, IPv4 vs IPv6 briefly, and how DNS works like a phone book (domain name → IP address) with a step-by-step trace of what happens when you type 'google.com,' (3) how data travels — packets, routers, and why data takes different routes (TCP/IP model explained with the postal system analogy), (4) TCP vs UDP — what each is used for and why the difference matters (video calls vs file downloads), (5) HTTP and HTTPS — what happens during an HTTP request-response cycle, what TLS/SSL encryption does and why the padlock matters, (6) how a web server works — what happens on the server side when your request arrives, static vs dynamic content, (7) browsers — how they render HTML/CSS/JS into what you see. Include a complete end-to-end trace: 'you press Enter → web page appears.' Tone: clear, layered, and technically accurate without overwhelming jargon.

Linked lists ko computer science beginners ke liye ek comprehensive aur visual Hindi tutorial mein explain karo. Niche diye topics include karo: (1) linked list kya hoti hai — arrays se comparison: memory allocation ka fark, contiguous vs non-contiguous storage, (2) node ka concept: data aur next pointer — ek train ke dabbon ki analogy se samjhao, (3) singly linked list ka structure: head node, null/None se end, Python mein Node class aur LinkedList class implement karo, (4) basic operations step by step code ke saath: insertion (beginning, end, middle), deletion (beginning, end, specific node), traversal aur search, (5) doubly linked list kya hoti hai — previous pointer add karna — kab ye zyada useful hoti hai, (6) circular linked list briefly — kab use hoti hai (OS scheduling, media players), (7) linked list vs array: kab kaunsa better hai — time complexity table ke saath (O(1) insertion vs O(n) search). Practice problems: reverse a linked list, detect a cycle, find the middle node — hints ke saath. Diagrams text mein describe karo.

Write a comprehensive, hands-on tutorial on CSS Flexbox for web development beginners who know basic HTML and CSS but struggle with layouts. Include: (1) why Flexbox exists — the problems with float-based and table-based layouts, (2) the flex container vs flex items concept — what display: flex does to children, (3) the main axis and cross axis explained visually — how flex-direction (row, column, row-reverse, column-reverse) changes them, (4) alignment properties in detail with examples: justify-content (main axis — flex-start, center, space-between, space-around, space-evenly), align-items (cross axis — stretch, center, flex-start, flex-end), align-content (multi-line), (5) flex item properties: flex-grow, flex-shrink, flex-basis — the flex shorthand explained intuitively with the 'growing and shrinking' analogy, (6) order property and align-self — overriding alignment for individual items, (7) three practical layout challenges solved with Flexbox: a centered card on the page, a navigation bar with logo left and links right, a responsive card grid. Provide complete HTML/CSS code for each. Include a Flexbox cheat sheet at the end. Tone: visual and project-focused.

input: backend development kya hota hai kaise seekhein
target: Backend web development ko Hindi mein ek structured guide mein explain karo un beginners ke liye jinhe frontend ki thodi jankari hai aur backend seekhna chahte hain. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) frontend vs backend vs full-stack — kya fark hai, backend kya kaam karta hai (data store karna, authentication, business logic), (2) backend ke liye language choose karna — Node.js (JavaScript), Python (Django/Flask), Java (Spring), PHP — beginners ke liye Node.js ya Python kyun best hai, (3) server kya hota hai — localhost se shuruwaat, HTTP request kaise receive karta hai server, (4) Node.js + Express se ek simple REST API banana: npm setup, ek GET aur POST route banana step by step, (5) database connection: MongoDB (NoSQL) ya PostgreSQL (SQL) — dono ka brief comparison aur Node.js se connect karna, (6) authentication basics: password hashing (bcrypt), JWT tokens kya hote hain, login/logout flow, (7) deployment: Render, Railway, ya Vercel pe free mein backend deploy karna. Learning roadmap: 0 se job-ready backend developer banne mein kitna waqt aur kya karna hai. Resources: The Odin Project, freeCodeCamp.

Write a clear, practical introduction to Docker for a developer who has heard of containers but has no idea what Docker actually does or why it matters. Include: (1) the core problem Docker solves — 'it works on my machine' — explain with a relatable scenario of code breaking in production but working locally, (2) what a container is — use the shipping container analogy: standardized box that holds everything the app needs regardless of what ship (server) it's on, (3) containers vs virtual machines — key differences in architecture, startup time, and resource usage, (4) core Docker concepts: images (blueprint), containers (running instance), Dockerfile (instructions to build an image), Docker Hub (registry), (5) step-by-step: write a simple Dockerfile for a Node.js app — FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, RUN, EXPOSE, CMD explained line by line, (6) essential Docker CLI commands: docker build, docker run, docker ps, docker stop, docker exec, docker logs — with practical examples, (7) Docker Compose basics — why you need it for multi-container apps (e.g., app + database), a simple docker-compose.yml example. Tone: practical, analogy-rich, and project-oriented.

C++ pointers ko beginners ke liye — jo basic C++ syntax jaante hain — ek detailed aur visual Hindi tutorial mein samjhao. Yeh ek topic hai jahan maximum log confuse hote hain, isliye extra clear rakho. Niche cover karo: (1) memory ka concept — RAM mein variables kaise store hote hain, har variable ka ek address hota hai — ghar ke address ki analogy, (2) pointer kya hota hai — ek variable jo doosre variable ka address store karta hai, declaration syntax: int* ptr, (3) & (address-of) aur * (dereference) operators — kya farq hai, code examples ke saath step by step, (4) pointer arithmetic — ptr++, ptr-- kya karte hain, array traversal pointer se kaise karte hain, (5) pointers aur functions: pass by value vs pass by reference — swap function example ke saath fark dikhao, (6) dynamic memory allocation: new aur delete operators — heap vs stack briefly, memory leak kya hota hai aur kaise hota hai, (7) null pointer, dangling pointer, aur wild pointer — yeh kya hain aur inse kaise bachein. Common errors: segmentation fault kab aata hai. Practice exercises with solutions.

Write a comprehensive guide on writing clean, maintainable code for junior developers who can make code work but whose code is messy, hard to read, or difficult to maintain. Base advice on established principles (Clean Code by Robert Martin, Google style guides). Include: (1) why clean code matters — the cost of messy code: debugging time, onboarding new team members, technical debt, (2) naming conventions — how to name variables, functions, classes, and files meaningfully — examples of bad names vs good names (data, x, temp → userEmailAddress, calculateTotalPrice), (3) functions — single responsibility principle: one function does one thing, ideal function length, avoid side effects, (4) comments — when to write them and when not to: good code should be self-documenting; comments explain 'why,' not 'what,' (5) DRY principle (Don't Repeat Yourself) — how to identify and refactor duplicated code with an example, (6) code formatting and consistency — indentation, line length, blank lines, and why automated formatters (Prettier, Black) are essential, (7) code review mindset — how to give and receive feedback, what to look for when reviewing someone else's code. Include a 'clean code checklist' at the end. Tone: direct, practical, and professional.

input: Django framework sikhna hai Python mein

Write a comprehensive tutorial on sorting algorithms for beginner-to-intermediate computer science students or self-taught developers preparing for technical interviews. Cover: (1) why sorting matters — real-world applications: search optimization, database indexing, rendering ranked results, (2) Bubble Sort: how it works step by step with a 5-element array trace, time complexity O(n²), why it's taught but never used in production, (3) Selection Sort: the 'find minimum and swap' approach — trace through an example, compare with Bubble Sort, (4) Insertion Sort: how it mimics sorting playing cards — trace an example, explain why it's efficient for nearly-sorted data, (5) Merge Sort: divide-and-conquer explained — recursive splitting and merging — trace example with call tree, O(n log n) time complexity derivation, (6) Quick Sort: pivot selection, partition logic — trace an example, average vs worst case performance, (7) when to use which: a practical decision guide — small arrays, nearly sorted data, stability requirements, memory constraints. Provide Python implementations for all six algorithms. Include a time/space complexity comparison table. Tone: educational, thorough, and interview-prep focused.

input: JavaScript promises aur async await samjhao
target: JavaScript mein asynchronous programming — Promises aur async/await — ko beginners ke liye Hindi mein clearly explain karo. Yeh topic bahut logon ko confuse karta hai, isliye simple analogies use karo. Niche diye topics cover karo: (1) synchronous vs asynchronous programming kya hota hai — ek restaurant analogy: synchronous = ek hi kaam karo, async = order leke next customer pe jao, (2) callbacks kya the — pehle async kaise karte the, callback hell problem kya hoti hai aur kyun yeh problematic hai, (3) Promise kya hota hai — teen states: pending, fulfilled, rejected — ek delivery tracking analogy, (4) Promise syntax: .then(), .catch(), .finally() — chaining ke saath example, (5) Promise.all(), Promise.race(), Promise.allSettled() — kab kaunsa use karein use cases ke saath, (6) async/await syntax — Promises ko aur readable kaise banata hai, try/catch ke saath error handling, (7) real-world example: fetch API se data lana using async/await — JSONPlaceholder se users fetch karo, error handle karo, data display karo. Common mistakes: await without async, unhandled promise rejection. Practice exercises bhi do.

input: what is machine learning for beginners
target: Write an accessible, accurate introduction to Machine Learning for a programmer who knows basic Python but has no ML or statistics background. Structure it as: (1) what machine learning actually is — not magic, but pattern recognition from data — contrast with traditional programming (rules given vs rules learned), (2) the three main types of ML: supervised learning (labeled data — classification and regression), unsupervised learning (unlabeled data — clustering), reinforcement learning (reward-based) — one simple example for each, (3) the ML workflow: data collection → preprocessing → feature engineering → model selection → training → evaluation → deployment, (4) key concepts explained simply: training/test split, overfitting and underfitting (with the 'memorizing vs understanding' analogy), bias-variance tradeoff, (5) Python ML ecosystem overview: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Scikit-learn — what each does and how they work together, (6) a complete beginner project walkthrough: predicting house prices with Scikit-learn's Linear Regression — from loading data to evaluating with RMSE, (7) where to go next: courses (Andrew Ng's ML Specialization on Coursera, fast.ai), datasets (Kaggle, UCI ML Repository). Competitive programming mein naye aane wale students ke liye ek comprehensive Hindi starter guide banao. input: data structures kaunse zaroori hain interview ke liye

input: how to build a mobile app for beginners
